relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Elihu continued speaking.
He called wise hearers to listen.
Words must be tested like food is tasted.
He asks the hearers to choose justice and discern good.
He summarizes Job as claiming righteousness while feeling denied justice.
He hears Job saying his wound is incurable despite no transgression.
Elihu sees Job as surrounded by mockery.
He thinks Job’s speech is moving in dangerous company.
He hears Job as saying devotion gains nothing.
Elihu insists injustice is far from the Divine.
He returns to deed and way as moral order.
Shaddai, he says, does not twist justice.
No one appointed the earth above God.
If God gathered back spirit and breath,
all flesh would return to dust.
Elihu asks for understanding ears.
He asks whether one opposed to justice could govern.
He invokes the One beyond kings and nobles.
Rich and poor are equally the work of divine hands.
The mighty can vanish in a moment.
Every way and step is seen.
No darkness hides harmful action.
God needs no court appointment from humans.
The mighty are broken and replaced.
Their works are known and overturned.
Judgment can become visible.
They turned aside from divine ways.
The cry of poor and afflicted reaches divine hearing.
Quiet and hidden face are beyond human control.
Elihu says corrupt rule is restrained so people are not trapped.
He imagines a humble confession of correction.
He urges being taught what is unseen.
He challenges Job to choose and speak what he knows.
He appeals again to wise hearers.
He judges Job’s words as lacking knowledge.
He wishes Job’s answer would be tested fully.
He says Job keeps adding words against the Divine.
